brands.name: Phenix Salon Suites
brands.notes: Salon-suite leasing concept (franchisees rent suites to independent beauty professionals); semi-absentee B2B model with 0-1 employees and no inventory. Royalty and ad fund are charged per square foot ($0.34/sq.ft. and $0.06/sq.ft. monthly) rather than as a percent of revenue, so royaltyPercent/adFundPercent are intentionally omitted.

riskFlags.title: Litigation history disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz's FDD review notes Phenix Salon Suites discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its Franchise Disclosure Document; prospective buyers should review Item 3 details. No major active franchisee litigation surfaced in public searches.

riskFlags.title: High investment relative to revenue
riskFlags.detail: Initial investment of $721K-$1.42M against a median reported unit gross revenue of roughly $426K; the model's economics rest on long-term suite-rental margins rather than top-line volume, so payback math differs from typical salon concepts.

brands.name: The Lash Lounge
brands.notes: Eyelash-extension salon franchise founded in Dallas; sold by Franworth to The Riverside Company in March 2024 as the anchor of Riverside's boutique beauty platform. Actively selling franchises in all US states per its franchise site.

riskFlags.title: Franchisor negative equity disclosed as special risk
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor reported a members' deficit (negative equity) of $3,055,433 and negative working capital of roughly $2.73M as of December 31, 2023, and its FDD flags this as a special risk that calls into question its financial ability to support franchisees.
riskFlags.source: FranPass FDD analysis of The Lash Lounge

riskFlags.title: Settled franchisee arbitration claims (2019-2020)
riskFlags.detail: Four litigation matters stemmed from arbitration claims filed by franchisees against predecessor The Lash Lounge Franchise, LLC in 2019, alleging misrepresented construction and pre-opening training costs and fraudulent misrepresentation; all settled in early 2020.
riskFlags.source: FranPass FDD analysis of The Lash Lounge

riskFlags.title: Ownership change in 2024
riskFlags.detail: Franworth sold The Lash Lounge to The Riverside Company in March 2024 in a recapitalization/restructuring; new ownership may change support, fees, and strategy versus historical FDDs.

nationwide.source: The Lash Lounge franchise site: 'growing quickly across the country, offering amazing opportunities in all states'
nationwide.note: Franchisor advertises availability in all US states

liquidCapitalMin.source: The Lash Lounge franchise site and Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

royaltyPercent.source: The Lash Lounge franchise site and Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory
royaltyPercent.note: Minimum royalty of $250/week applies after second year of operation

adFundPercent.source: The Lash Lounge franchise site and Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory
adFundPercent.note: Plus required local advertising spend (~$1,500-$2,000/month in year one per 2025 FDD summaries)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter FDD Talk 2024 review of The Lash Lounge (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2024/06/10/fdd-talk-the-lash-lounge-franchise-costs-fees-average-revenues-and-or-profits-2024-review/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales of franchised salons for calendar 2023 per Item 19 of the 2024 FDD; 2025 FDD discloses 2024 revenue for 121 salons by quartile

fddAvailable.source: Research and Markets - The Lash Lounge Franchise Disclosure Document

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 review (2025 FDD Item 19 includes 2024 revenue by quartile for 121 salons)

veteranDiscount.note: $2,500 off franchise fee or $10,000 off development fee

multiUnitAvailable.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (multi-unit development fee incentive disclosed)

brands.name: Scissors & Scotch
brands.notes: Upscale men's grooming franchise (barbershop + spa amenities + private bar/lounge) founded in Omaha in 2015. Young but fast-growing system actively seeking franchise partners nationwide.

riskFlags.title: Young, small franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2017 with roughly 33 open units; limited operating history versus mature grooming brands, though no lawsuits or bankruptcy are disclosed in its FDD per Vetted Biz's review.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz FDD review; Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: High investment and staffing needs for the category
riskFlags.detail: Initial investment of $631,800-$845,250 with 10-25 employees required is at the high end for hair-care franchises, reflecting the built-in bar/lounge component (which also entails local liquor licensing).

nationwide.source: Scissors & Scotch franchise site: 'looking to expand nationwide, and are seeking business partners to join us'
nationwide.note: Franchisor states nationwide US expansion intent; no excluded states published

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Sharpsheets FDD summary
franchiseFee.note: $40,000 for second unit and $20,000 for each additional under multi-unit agreements

royaltyPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz FDD review
royaltyPercent.note: Tiered royalty of 4%-6.5% of gross sales, rising with sales volume; 4% is the starting/base tier

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average annual gross sales per location derived from FDD Item 19

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Scissors & Scotch FDD on file)

item19Available.source: Sharpsheets FDD analysis (Item 19 gross sales disclosed)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Sharpsheets FDD summary (multi-unit agreement fee schedule)

brands.name: Minuteman Press
brands.notes: B2B print, design and marketing-services franchise operating since 1973 with 1,000+ locations in 5 countries; ranked #1 in Entrepreneur's Printing/Marketing Services category for 20+ consecutive years. Distinctive royalty cap: 6% royalties apply only up to a fixed monthly sales level, with no ad fund fee.

riskFlags.title: Landmark 1998 FTC judgment for deceptive earnings claims
riskFlags.detail: After a six-month trial, the US District Court (E.D.N.Y.) found Minuteman Press International, affiliate Speedy Sign-A-Rama, and officers including chairman Roy Titus liable for false and unsubstantiated earnings and profit claims made to prospective franchisees (FTC v. Minuteman Press, 53 F. Supp. 2d 248). Decades old, but a landmark franchise-disclosure case tied to this franchisor.
riskFlags.source: Federal Trade Commission press release / Justia

riskFlags.title: Ongoing franchisee complaints documented
riskFlags.detail: Unhappy Franchisee maintains a long-running Minuteman Press complaint thread where former and current franchisees allege issues including equipment leasing costs and disputes over resale/transfer; individual claims are unverified but the volume is notable.

nationwide.source: Minuteman Press franchise FAQ: 'Select areas are available across the U.S.' plus international markets
nationwide.note: Sells across the US, though specific territories are subject to availability

totalInvestmentMin.note: Lower end reflects equipment leasing; buying equipment outright raises the range (~$161K-$221K per Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz FDD review
franchiseFee.note: Fee ranges $35,000-$48,500 depending on market/territory; $35,000 is the low end of the standard new-franchisee range

royaltyPercent.source: Minuteman Press franchise FAQ; Vetted Biz FDD review
royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross monthly sales, capped at a fixed monthly sales level - no royalties on sales above the cap

adFundPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz FDD review (no national ad fund fee)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz Minuteman Press FDD review (https://www.vettedbiz.com/franchises/minuteman-press)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average 2024 gross sales of US franchised centers per FDD Item 19; gross revenue, not profit

totalUnits.note: All franchised; operates in US, Canada, UK, Australia, South Africa

minFootprint.source: Minuteman Press franchise FAQ (typical new center ~1,000 sq ft)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Minuteman Press FDD on file)

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz FDD review (Item 19 average gross sales disclosed)

veteranDiscount.note: $10,000 off licensing fee for new locations; $5,000 off existing locations

canRunFromHome.source: Minuteman Press franchise FAQ; Entrepreneur directory

isGrowing.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (+6.3% unit growth); ASI: topped 1,000 locations and $630M system sales in 2024

brands.name: Liberty Tax
brands.notes: Retail income-tax preparation franchise founded by John Hewitt in 1997; one of the largest US tax-prep franchises but the system has contracted significantly and its parent (NextPoint Financial) went through bankruptcy in 2023. Low entry cost but the 14% royalty plus 5% advertising fee is among the highest combined fee loads in franchising.

riskFlags.title: Parent company bankruptcy (2023)
riskFlags.detail: Parent NextPoint Financial Inc. filed CCAA proceedings in Canada and Chapter 15 in Delaware in July 2023 with ~$284M in debt; its assets, including Liberty Tax, were sold to lender groups under court supervision. Franchised locations were not part of the proceedings, but ownership and capital structure changed via distressed sale.

riskFlags.title: DOJ tax-fraud enforcement against franchisees and 2019 consent settlement
riskFlags.detail: Between 2013 and 2018 the DOJ filed 10 civil enforcement actions against Liberty Tax franchisees (including 'Elite 18' operators) for fabricating income, dependents, and credits on customer returns; ~70% of 28,000 IRS audits of Liberty customer returns (TY2012-2017) required corrections. A 2019 DOJ settlement imposed compliance obligations and barred founder/ex-CEO John Hewitt from the business.

riskFlags.title: System contraction
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's 2026 directory shows 1,969 units with a -24% unit-count trend, down from ~2,500+ locations historically; the company closed dozens of offices during the 2017-2019 fraud fallout and the network has kept shrinking.

riskFlags.title: High ongoing fees vs industry norms
riskFlags.detail: 14% royalty on gross receipts (with annual minimums from year two) plus a 5% advertising fee is a 19% combined fee load - far above the 6-10% typical for service franchises - on a relatively low average unit volume (~$132K).
riskFlags.source: Liberty Tax franchise investment page; Sharpsheets FDD analysis

nationwide.source: Liberty Tax franchise investment page: franchises sold to residents of any jurisdiction where registration/filing requirements are met
nationwide.note: Actively recruiting US-wide, subject to state franchise registration

royaltyPercent.note: 14% of gross receipts with annual minimums ($5K year 2, $8K year 3, $11K year 4+)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Sharpsheets Liberty Tax Service FDD analysis (https://sharpsheets.io/blog/liberty-tax-service-franchise-fdd-profits-costs/)

brands.name: Plato's Closet
brands.notes: Resale (buy-sell-trade) teen and young-adult apparel retailer franchised by Winmark Corporation (NASDAQ: WINA) since 1999, with 525+ stores in the US and Canada and a 99% franchisee renewal rate. Marketing fee is a flat annual amount (~$1,500/year per Entrepreneur) rather than a percent of sales, so adFundPercent is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosures in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz's review notes Plato's Closet discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Item 3/4 history); no significant active franchisee litigation or closure waves surfaced in public searches, and Winmark reports a 99% franchise renewal rate.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz FDD review; Winmark Franchises

nationwide.source: Winmark Franchises Plato's Closet page: 525+ locations across North America with open territories listed on its Available Territories page
nationwide.note: Recruiting across the US and Canada; specific open territories listed on Winmark's available-territories page

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review
franchiseFee.note: Reduced to $15,000 for second/subsequent stores or existing Winmark-brand franchisees

royaltyPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review
royaltyPercent.note: Plus flat annual marketing fee (~$1,500) and required local marketing spend

avgUnitRevenue.source: Winmark Franchises Plato's Closet page citing FDD Item 19 (https://www.winmarkfranchises.com/platos-closet/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales across 492 stores open at least one year, per Item 19 of the current Plato's Closet FDD; top quartile averaged $2,033,682

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Direct Plato's Closet UFOC/FDD listing

item19Available.source: Winmark Franchises Plato's Closet page (publishes Item 19 averages)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (reduced $15,000 fee for second/subsequent stores)
